_meta.note: Curriculum references follow pattern: '[Subject] curriculum for [class-range] Page [X]'. Add textbook references in textbooks.json
_meta.primary_format: Weekly table: DAYS | PHASE 1 STARTER 10mins | PHASE 2 MAIN 40mins | PHASE 3 REFLECTION 10mins
_meta.jhs_format: Single lesson: Phase/Duration | Learner Activities | Resources
_meta.variation_pool: Each entry has multiple strand/topic options so AI generates different content every session

KG1._pedagogy: Play-based, song and rhyme-driven, movement activities, very simple language, 3 days only (Mon/Wed/Fri)

English Language.perfIndicator: Learners listen attentively and follow simple two-step instructions
Learners recite familiar rhymes with correct rhythm and actions
Learners identify words that sound the same at the beginning
Learners identify and name uppercase letters of the alphabet
Learners trace and draw basic lines, curves and shapes as pre-writing preparation

Mathematics.perfIndicator: Learners count objects from 1 to 5 using one-to-one correspondence
Learners recognise and name numerals 1 to 5
Learners identify and name circle, square and triangle from everyday objects
Learners count objects from 1 to 10 and match numerals to quantities

Creative Arts and Design.perfIndicator: Learners explore and manipulate different art materials to create simple artworks
Learners participate in music and movement activities using simple rhythm instruments

Our World Our People (OWOP).perfIndicator: Learners identify and name the major parts of the body
Learners identify and name members of their immediate family

Religious and Moral Education (RME).perfIndicator: Learners name living and non-living things that God created in their environment

Ghanaian Language.perfIndicator: Learners exchange greetings in the morning, afternoon and evening in their Ghanaian Language

Physical Education.perfIndicator: Learners demonstrate basic locomotor movements such as walking, running and jumping

KG2._pedagogy: Play-based, story-driven, imaginative play, 3 days only

English Language.perfIndicator: Learners listen to a short story and answer simple questions about characters and events
Learners identify and produce the sounds of consonants b, c, d, f, g
Learners copy their name and simple three-letter words clearly

Mathematics.perfIndicator: Learners count objects from 1 to 10 and compare groups using more, less, or equal
Learners combine two groups of objects and state the total
Learners compare and order objects by size and length using words such as long, short, tall

B1._pedagogy: Guided discovery, colourful charts, simple language, games, 5 days

English Language.perfIndicator: Learners listen attentively and respond to simple oral texts
Learners identify and produce rhyming words in songs and poems
Learners identify the letters of the alphabet and produce their sounds
Learners read and write common sight words correctly
Learners write letters of the alphabet legibly using correct strokes
Learners identify and use nouns to name people, places and things
Learners construct simple sentences using familiar vocabulary

Mathematics.perfIndicator: Learners count objects up to 10 and represent them with numerals
Learners add two single-digit numbers using objects and pictures
Learners subtract single-digit numbers using objects and number lines
Learners identify and describe basic 2D shapes in the environment
Learners compare and order lengths using non-standard units

Science.perfIndicator: Learners distinguish between living and non-living things by their characteristics
Learners identify and name the basic parts of a plant
Learners describe the different types of soil and their uses
Learners describe and sort materials based on properties such as hard, soft, rough and smooth

Our World Our People (OWOP).perfIndicator: Learners describe themselves using personal details such as name, age, family and community
Learners identify and describe the roles of community helpers in their environment

Religious and Moral Education (RME).perfIndicator: Learners identify and name things God created in the environment
Learners demonstrate good behaviour such as saying please, thank you and sorry

Creative Arts and Design.perfIndicator: Learners explore and create artworks using materials found in their environment
Learners participate in traditional Ghanaian songs and dances

Ghanaian Language.perfIndicator: Learners listen and respond to greetings and simple conversations in Ghanaian Language
Learners read and identify familiar words in their Ghanaian Language

Physical Education.perfIndicator: Learners demonstrate basic locomotor movements and explain their importance to health
Learners participate in simple traditional Ghanaian games and describe the rules

History.perfIndicator: Learners narrate the history of their family using information from grandparents and parents
Learners describe important events and festivals celebrated in their community

Computing (ICT).perfIndicator: Learners identify and name common computing devices used at home and at school

B4._pedagogy: Learner-centred, group work, problem-solving, real-life Ghanaian contexts, 5 days

English Language.perfIndicator: Learners match sounds to their corresponding letter patterns
Learners identify and discuss values embedded in songs and rhymes
Learners use common rhyming words and digraphs to decode unfamiliar words
Learners identify and use common and proper nouns in sentences
Learners write structured paragraphs using appropriate modes of writing
Learners apply commas and other punctuation marks correctly in sentences
Learners read age-appropriate texts and answer questions to demonstrate comprehension
Learners identify and use personal pronouns correctly in oral and written communication
Learners participate in conversations using appropriate vocabulary and sentence structures
Learners write narrative texts with a clear beginning, middle and end

Mathematics.perfIndicator: Learners model number quantities and place values for multi-digit numbers up to 100,000
Learners identify numbers in different positions and compare whole numbers up to 10,000
Learners add and subtract multi-digit numbers with and without regrouping
Learners multiply two-digit numbers by one-digit numbers using various strategies
Learners identify and represent fractions as parts of a whole using models and diagrams
Learners measure and estimate lengths using standard units (cm, m, km)
Learners measure and compare masses using standard units (g, kg)
Learners describe and classify 2D shapes based on their properties
Learners collect data and represent it using pictographs and bar charts

Science.perfIndicator: Learners classify animals into insects, birds, mammals and reptiles based on observable characteristics
Learners describe the life processes of animals including movement, feeding and reproduction
Learners identify the parts of a flowering plant and explain their functions
Learners investigate and describe the physical properties of common materials
Learners investigate reversible and irreversible changes in materials
Learners identify effects of heat on the change of state of substances
Learners describe how natural resources are used and explain the importance of conservation

Our World Our People (OWOP).perfIndicator: Learners explain how each individual is uniquely and specially created by God
Learners describe how individuals differ from one another in appearance, talent and ability
Learners describe the structure and functions of Ghana's democratic government
Learners explain the importance of environmental protection and identify ways to protect the environment

Religious and Moral Education (RME).perfIndicator: Learners describe the attributes of God as creator using examples from their religious traditions
Learners demonstrate moral values such as honesty, respect and tolerance in daily interactions
Learners explain the significance of prayer and worship in different religious traditions in Ghana

Creative Arts and Design.perfIndicator: Learners study and discuss the performing artworks of selected Ghanaian artists
Learners create original artworks inspired by Ghanaian cultural practices and symbols
Learners plan and present a short cultural performance using music, dance or drama

Ghanaian Language.perfIndicator: Learners tell and retell familiar Ghanaian folktales using appropriate vocabulary and expression
Learners read passages in their Ghanaian Language and answer comprehension questions
Learners write simple sentences in their Ghanaian Language using correct grammar

Physical Education.perfIndicator: Learners participate in traditional Ghanaian games and explain their cultural significance
Learners perform fitness exercises and explain how they improve health and well-being
Learners demonstrate correct techniques for running and jumping activities

Computing (ICT).perfIndicator: Learners identify and describe the parts of a computer and their functions
Learners use word processing software to create, edit and format simple documents
Learners explain the importance of internet safety and demonstrate safe online practices

History.subStrand: Why and How We Study History
History.perfIndicator: Learners explain the importance of studying the history of Ghana for national identity and unity
Learners describe the location, organisation and achievements of pre-colonial Ghanaian states
Learners identify and describe the major ethnic groups in Ghana and their cultural traditions

French Language.perfIndicator: Learners exchange greetings and introduce themselves in French using appropriate vocabulary
Learners count from 1 to 20 and identify colours in French

B7._pedagogy: Analytical, critical thinking, peer assessment, group projects, real-world problem solving
B7._format: JHS single-lesson format: Phase/Duration | Learner Activities | Resources

English Language.perfIndicator: Learners listen actively and respond accurately to spoken texts on diverse topics and issues
Learners apply reading strategies to comprehend, analyse and evaluate grade-level texts

English Language.subStrand: Parts of Speech — Nouns and Pronouns
English Language.perfIndicator: Learners identify and apply nouns, pronouns and their functions in oral and written communication
Learners identify and use prepositions correctly in sentences and written texts
Learners compose formal letters following the correct format and appropriate register
Learners compose informal letters using appropriate tone, vocabulary and format
Learners analyse literary elements such as plot, theme and characterisation in prose texts

Mathematics.perfIndicator: Learners apply understanding of rational and irrational numbers in real-life problem solving situations
Learners add, subtract, multiply and divide fractions and mixed numbers
Learners identify, describe and extend number and geometric patterns using algebraic reasoning
Learners investigate and describe properties of geometric shapes and their transformations

Science.perfIndicator: Learners investigate and describe the physical and chemical properties of matter using experiments
Learners describe the structure and functions of cells as the basic unit of life
Learners describe the structure of the Earth and explain the causes of earthquakes and volcanoes

Social Studies.perfIndicator: Learners describe the location of Ghana using cardinal directions, coordinates and map features
Learners analyse Ghanaian cultural practices and explain their significance in promoting national identity

Religious and Moral Education (RME).perfIndicator: Learners analyse the attributes of God as revealed in different religious texts and traditions

Creative Arts and Design.perfIndicator: Learners analyse and evaluate Ghanaian artworks for their cultural, historical and aesthetic significance
Creative Arts and Design.reference: Creative Arts and Design CCP B7-B9 Pg. 2

Computing (ICT).perfIndicator: Learners distinguish between hardware and software components and explain their functions in a computing system
Learners write simple programs using block-based coding to solve problems

Career Technology.perfIndicator: Learners identify career pathways related to technology and explain their relevance to Ghana's development

French Language.perfIndicator: Learners exchange greetings and personal information in French using appropriate vocabulary and expressions

Ghanaian Language.perfIndicator: Learners engage in meaningful conversations in their Ghanaian Language on familiar topics

Physical Education.perfIndicator: Learners demonstrate improved levels of physical fitness through regular exercise and explain the health benefits
